creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users can ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion without SQL knowledge. The LLM generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cost-effective.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard from Google Chrome that replaces DOM parsing and screenshot-based navigation with structured, named tools that agents call directly. It offers both declarative HTML attribute APIs and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering custom agent-callable actions, currently in early preview on Chrome 146.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser describes a four-layer system for asynchronous AI-assisted development that keeps humans available while agents work autonomously. The stack includes signal agents for context management, verification gates for safety, self-improvement loops via conversation history analysis, and holistic monitoring like sleep tracking to prevent burnout.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: A 4B parameter model finetuned with RL to use a get_table_name tool and self-correct SQL queries outperforms a 235B model that hallucinates answers. The technique emphasizes tool discipline and behavioral training over model scaling, achieving results on FinQA with training costs under 500 dollars.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin analyzes Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model withdrawal following a US government export-control directive allegedly tied to jailbreak concerns. The video evaluates whether Fable 5 lived up to hype relative to Opus 4.8 based on pre-withdrawal benchmarks and discusses implications for developers building on these models.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that enables rapid building and deployment of production-grade AI agents. It provides agent identity management, sandboxed code execution, audit trails, and integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin hosts a live AI Q&A stream on Saturdays covering AI coding, agents, and Claude Code. The session combines discussion with casual chess play and invites community participation around AI-related topics.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow that routes frontend development tasks to Claude Opus for planning and integration logic, and Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design and code generation. The approach leverages each model's relative strengths while reducing overall inference costs.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving systems through autonomous feedback loops. Covers use cases including autonomous ads optimization and SEO, with emphasis on memory and tool integration to enable continuous improvement.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the state of autonomous code generation and agents, presenting features like /goal and /Mission for managing extended coding tasks. He demonstrates prompt strategies and tools like Crewlet and Superdesign that enable autonomous development workflows.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason presents OpenAI Symphony as a new AI coding paradigm with harness setup, auto testing, and workflow management capabilities. The video covers how Symphony works, tool integration setup, and workflow configuration through WORKFLOW.md.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses autonomous agent systems including Autoresearch, AutoAgent, and Claude Code with multi-layer memory. The video covers self-evolving mechanisms in Hermes and Openclaw, comparing skill acquisition approaches across different agent frameworks.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three variants: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid mamba transformer, latent mixture of experts, and MTP in their architecture to differentiate from other open-source models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a large language model that adopts sparse attention (MSA) instead of full attention for improved inference efficiency. The model includes optimized KV cache handling and tiled I/O improvements that reduce latency during prefill and decode stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is an agent framework that enables flexible integration through a "harness" layer. The video compares Pi's architecture against other coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Antigravity, examining how products incorporate increasingly complex harness mechanisms.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code analyzes Huawei's announced Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the US-China semiconductor race. The video discusses how EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding are shaping advances toward smaller chip nodes, while examining implications of accelerating hardware competition.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses strategies for tech job hunting in an AI-driven market, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and open source contributions. The video argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI automation and uses transportation industry evolution as an analogy for job market transformation.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production best practices for deploying AI agents: streaming tool call events for responsiveness, layered authentication via API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost budgets through lifecycle hooks and model routing, and observability via metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. These patterns apply across frameworks and cloud providers.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A tutorial on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python to build a serverless REST API. The video demonstrates both manual console setup and automated deployment using CDK for DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway resources.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: Five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations in agent systems: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardians as code-based rules, and runtime guardrails for agent self-correction. These methods reduce token waste, improve accuracy, and prevent silent failures.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video discussing whether Google engineers use "vibe coding" practices, with comparisons between OpenAI Codex and Claude Code, and speculation about code generation adoption at Google in 2024-2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company whose practices the creator frames as potentially ending open source development. The video explores how Malus operates and whether its approach represents a threat to open source maintainers and the broader ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform enables unified data visibility across finance, supply chain, HR, and sales functions. She argues that enterprise-scale planning spreadsheets fail at scale, advocates for building complete platforms rather than single features, and emphasizes hiring discipline and trusted customer validation as keys to competing against established players.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early users are not proof of real product-market fit and that founders often mistake enthusiasm for actual demand. He contends that true PMF manifests as pull (customers asking prices) rather than push (requests to stay updated), and recommends embedded customer engagement over formal interviews to assess market validation.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization is the future for AI tools, contrary to 2024 VC consensus favoring subscriptions. He identifies human trust adoption as the true bottleneck for AI agents, not technological limitations.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapping playbook after reaching $10M ARR with no external funding. The interview covers strategies for hitting $1M ARR, differences between early and later-stage growth, churn reduction, pricing experimentation, and decision-making frameworks for founders.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
